project_name: MySQL to BigQuery Data Ingestion Pipeline

columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: Unique employee identifier. Source PK. decimal(10,0) has no fractional part; INT64 is the idiomatic BigQuery integer type.

columns.name: first_name
columns.description: Employee first name. BigQuery STRING is variable-length; no max-length constraint required.

columns.name: email
columns.description: Employee email address. NOT NULL in source; null-constraint validation must be applied pre-load.

columns.name: phone_number
columns.description: Employee phone number. Nullable in source; NULLs must be preserved.

columns.name: hire_date
columns.description: Date the employee was hired. Direct DATE-to-DATE mapping; no timezone conversion required.

columns.name: job_id
columns.description: Job role code. Cluster column 2 of 3; frequently used in WHERE filters for job-level analytics.

columns.name: salary
columns.description: Employee salary with two decimal places. BigQuery NUMERIC (29 digits, 9 decimal places) safely accommodates decimal(8,2).

columns.name: commission_pct
columns.description: Commission percentage. Nullable — NULL indicates the employee is not on a commission plan.

columns.name: manager_id
columns.description: Self-referential FK to employee_id identifying the employee's direct manager. NULL for top-level employees (e.g. CEO).

columns.name: department_id
columns.description: Department identifier. Cluster column 1 of 3; highest-selectivity cluster key for department-level filtering.

columns.name: status
columns.description: Employment status (e.g. ACTIVE, INACTIVE). Cluster column 3 of 3; low-cardinality, frequently used in WHERE predicates.

columns.name: created_date
columns.description: Record creation timestamp. Partition column (DAY granularity). NOT NULL; used as primary time-range partition key and historical load boundary.

columns.name: updated_date
columns.description: Record last-update timestamp. Nullable. Used as the high-watermark delta key for incremental load tracking; pipeline queries MAX(updated_date) from BigQuery to identify changed records in MySQL.

cardinality.notes: Many employees can share the same manager (manager_id references employee_id on the same table). Top-level employees carry a NULL manager_id. Self-join is the only inferable relationship within the single-table source schema.
